prose:
Method–provenance block  
• Property: atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), f⁰ (average), and the condensed dual descriptor for acrolein (C₃H₄O).  
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting, exact 4-center integrals).  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (`acrolein.xyz`); single-point population analysis only (no re-optimization).  
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  
• Total system charge 0, spin multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states treated at doublet (M = 2) with the same functional/basis.  
• Software/backend: PySCF 2.x invoked via chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill.  
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = “ok”, trustworthy = true.  
  Warnings returned by chemkit (VERBATIM):
    – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – common header present  
  No other warnings or fall-backs reported.  
• Live engine log: the PySCF/chemkit run was streamed in real time to the `.out` file created by the skill (path announced at launch) and can be inspected for full SCF and population details.  
• Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp00pgixty/live.json

The headline value above (0.64232102) is the largest electrophilic Fukui index. The calculation was executed exactly as requested with B3LYP/def2-TZVP in the gas phase on the supplied geometry using chemkit’s `fukui-reactivity` (PySCF) tool.
